steps.name: Domain features (V3+)
steps.description: drought_index, water_balance, threshold flags, growth stage interactions, mulching effects

steps.name: V4+ domain features
steps.description: evapotranspiration proxy, soil stress, total water input

steps.name: V11 features
steps.description: decimal digits, field-normalized, groupby agg, pairwise cats

steps.name: Target encoding (per-fold, deferred)
steps.description: Per-fold target encoding for 6 categoricals + 10 pairwise (applied in CV loop)

steps.name: Feature interactions
steps.description: Ratios, products, diffs for key pairs

approvals: Prediction count matches test set: 270000 rows
Prediction distribution is consistent with training data
Best model 'logit_bias_tuned' achieves good balanced accuracy: 0.9726
Top 5 features: Temperature_C, Previous_Irrigation_mm, Wind_Speed_kmh, Soil_pH, Soil_Moisture
No missing values in training data

changes: Baseline: 4 models + weighted ensemble
23 features
Address severe class imbalance
Add target encoding for categorical features
Tune LightGBM hyperparameters
Add polynomial and binning features
Use stacking instead of weighted average
Replace logistic regression in ensemble
Add drought_index (Temp*Wind/(Moisture+1)) — huge class separator
Add water_balance (Rainfall+PrevIrrig-Temp*10)
Add Crop_Growth_Stage interaction features
Add binary threshold features (moisture<20, temp>30, wind>12)
Increase LightGBM num_leaves=127, max_depth=10
Increase XGBoost max_depth=9
Remove low-importance freq/target encodings
Keep V2 hyperparams (num_leaves=63, max_depth=8) — V3 deeper trees overfit
Keep V3 domain features (drought_index, water_balance, thresholds, stage interactions)
Add evapotranspiration proxy (Temp*Sunlight/Humidity)
Add soil_stress = Soil_pH * EC / (Organic_Carbon + 0.1)
Restore all frequency encodings (V3 removal didn't help)
Lower learning rate to 0.01 with more trees (3000) for LGBM
Add early stopping via more estimators but lower lr
Keep V4 features (67 features, domain + interactions)
Add CatBoost with native categorical handling
Multi-seed LGBM (seeds 42, 123, 777) averaged for stable predictions
Full 5-fold CV, all models
Revert learning rate to 0.01 (V4 proved lr=0.01 > lr=0.05)
Increase n_estimators: LGBM 5000, XGB 4000, CatBoost 5000 (compensate lower lr)
Drop features with importance < 1% of max (25 noisy features from V5)
Add rank-based features for top 5 numeric cols (percentile transforms)
Increase regularization (reg_alpha=0.3, reg_lambda=2.0, l2_leaf_reg=5.0)
Add 3-way interaction: moisture * temp * wind (drought severity)
CatBoost depth=8 with l2_leaf_reg=5.0 for better generalization
Use V2's winning lr=0.02 with n_estimators=2000 (proven optimal)
Use V2's moderate regularization: reg_alpha=0.1, reg_lambda=1.0
Use V2's moderate complexity: num_leaves=63, max_depth=8
Fix target encoding leakage: per-fold encoding instead of full-train
Keep proven V3 domain features: drought_index, water_balance, threshold flags, growth stage interactions
Keep proven V4 domain features: evapotranspiration, soil_stress, total_water_input
Remove StandardScaler (unnecessary for tree models)
Remove V6 rank features and 3-way interaction (added noise)
Fix weighted ensemble to compute actual OOF balanced accuracy
Keep V7 foundation: lr=0.02, per-fold TE, V2 regularization
Increase num_leaves to 80 (more splits for minority class boundaries)
Reduce min_child_samples to 30 (allow smaller leaf nodes for HIGH class)
Increase n_estimators: LGBM 2500, XGB 2000, CatBoost 2500
Add HIGH-class-targeted features: dry_hot_active, moisture_deficit_ratio, rainfall_adequacy
Drop 3 useless features (importance < 100): wind_high, Mulching_Used_freq, moisture_low
Exclude Mulching_Used from target encoding (importance=26)
Increase HIGH class weight by 1.5x in sample_weights
Use finer blend grid (step=0.05 instead of 0.1) for optimized_blend
Revert LGBM/XGB to V7 hyperparams (num_leaves=63, min_child_samples=50) — V8 relaxation hurt
Remove HIGH class weight boost (V8's 1.5x was too aggressive, -0.00137 on LGBM)
Keep V8 features: dry_hot_active, moisture_deficit_ratio, rainfall_adequacy
Keep V8 feature drops and Mulching_Used TE exclusion
CatBoost: depth=6, iterations=2000 (V7 params — depth=7 was slower AND worse)
Add post-blend probability calibration: search optimal HIGH class scaling on OOF
Keep finer blend grid (step=0.05)
Remove probability calibration entirely (source of CV-LB gap: 0.96999 CV vs 0.96715 LB)
Replace grid-searched blend with fixed score-proportional weights
Multi-seed averaging: LGBM 2 seeds, CatBoost 2 seeds
Add 3 ratio features: moisture_rainfall_ratio, temp_humidity_ratio, wind_temp_product
Drop temp_high, no_mulch (redundant with continuous/categorical originals)
Exclude Soil_Type from TE (low importance across all models)
Increase TE smoothing from 10 to 20 (reduce leakage)
Increase regularization: reg_alpha=0.15, reg_lambda=1.5, l2_leaf_reg=5.0
Append original dataset (miadul/irrigation-water-requirement-prediction-dataset) with sample_weight=0.35
Pairwise categorical interaction features with per-fold TE (C(8,2)=28 pairs → ~135 TE features)
Decimal digit extraction for all numeric columns (synthetic data exploit)
GroupBy aggregation features: groupby(cat)[num].agg(mean/std) for top combinations
Multi-class target encoding: P(class=k|category) for each of 3 classes (replaces broken single-value TE)
CatBoost native categorical handling via cat_features parameter
10-fold CV (up from 5-fold) for more stable OOF estimates
Logit-space bias tuning with nested CV: per-class logit offsets for balanced accuracy
CatBoost-dominant blend: CatBoost=0.65, LightGBM=0.25, XGBoost=0.10
Fix CatBoost sample_weight bug (was missing from fit call)
Fix LightGBM bagging_freq=1 (enable actual row subsampling)
Add field-normalized features: Previous_Irrigation/hectare, Rainfall/hectare
Baseline re-run via new autonomous pipeline — intentionally minimal to validate end-to-end (repo → Kaggle → PR) plumbing
Features: s6e4_threshold_booleans only (soil<25, temp>30, rain<300, wind>10) + label_encode of 6 original categoricals
Model: single LightGBM (num_leaves=31, lr=0.05, 2000 rounds early-stopped) — NO TE, NO ratios, NO ensembling, NO original-data augmentation
Training metric was plain accuracy (0.98561); balanced_accuracy (0.96360) computed post-hoc from OOF vs y_true
Public LB: 0.96152 — CV→LB gap of only 0.00208, CV is a reliable proxy
Regression vs V11 (-0.00896) is driven almost entirely by High-class recall collapsing to 91.9% — V8+ class-weight and V11 logit-bias tricks are specifically what closes that gap
Confirms: on this data, the minority-class engineering from V8–V11 is worth ~1% balanced-acc; threshold rules alone are insufficient
Port V11's highest-leverage techniques into the new pipeline, isolated to measure each effect
Class-balanced sample_weights (sklearn 'balanced' formula) passed to LightGBM — the High-class targeted technique from V8
Per-fold multiclass target encoding: for each of the 6 categoricals, add P(y=k | category) for k ∈ {High, Low, Medium}, smoothing=10; runs INSIDE the CV loop so no val/test leak
9 weather/soil interaction features: ratios, products, diffs (moisture×temp, rain×humidity, rain-prev_irrig, etc.) — mirrors V10's interaction set
LGBM hparams held close to V12 (num_leaves=63, lr=0.04, 2500 rounds, early_stop=100) to isolate feature/weight gains from hparam tuning
HIGH recall: 0.919 → 0.931 (+0.012), delivering the expected balanced-acc lift of +0.0034 vs V12
Still -0.0056 vs V11: V11 also has 10-fold CV, CatBoost-dominant blend across 3 model families, miadul-original-dataset augmentation, and logit-bias tuning — roadmap for V14+
Three-model CV blend: LGBM + XGB + CatBoost trained on V13's feature set (balanced weights, per-fold multiclass TE, 9 interactions)
Per-model CV scores: LGBM 0.96697 — XGB 0.96714 — CatBoost 0.96987 (strongest single model to date, approaching V11's 0.97256)
Score-proportional blend weights landed near-equal (0.333/0.333/0.334) because raw CV scores are all within 0.003 of each other

KEY INSIGHT: equal-weight blend (0.96812) is WORSE than CatBoost alone (0.96987). Blending dragged the strongest model down — V11's hand-tuned CatBoost=0.65/LGBM=0.25/XGB=0.10 was non-trivially better than uniform averaging
HIGH recall: 0.931 → 0.935 (+0.004), Low/Medium essentially unchanged
Runtime 74 min (V13: 25 min) — still comfortably under Kaggle's 9-hr cap so room for 10-fold in V15
V15 will address the blend-weight issue: either OOF-optimized weights via scipy or keeping CatBoost as the solo model with V15's original-data augmentation added on top
CatBoost-solo (best single model per V14 analysis) + 10-fold stratified CV + miadul external-dataset augmentation with sample_weight=0.35
640k train rows (630k comp + ~10k miadul after test-row dedupe)
HIGH recall 0.935 → 0.944 (+0.009) — external data helped the minority class learn cleaner boundaries

Score matches V14's CatBoost-alone (0.96987) within noise — miadul augmentation gave negligible lift because Kaggle generated the comp data FROM miadul, so the 'external' rows were largely redundant with train
10-fold std 0.00206 (V14 5-fold std 0.00081) — smaller folds are noisier; 10-fold's value here was stability of the OOF estimate for V16's bias-tuning, not direct CV lift
Roughly matches the CatBoost-solo ceiling on this feature set. Remaining gap to V11 (-0.003) is concentrated in the post-processing V16 handles
V15 config + postprocess: logit_bias — nested-CV Nelder-Mead per-class logit offsets on OOF probabilities
Bias vector converged to [0.0, 0.0, 0.00025] — essentially zero. delta_nested = 0.00000
Pre-bias bal-acc 0.96978, post-bias (full-fit) 0.96978 — identical

FINDING: logit-bias post-processing gives ZERO lift on an already class_weight-balanced model. V11's 0.97256 gain is NOT from logit-bias alone — must come from other techniques (multi-model blend, original-data augmentation, TE variants)
Not submitted (decision gate: delta_nested must be > 0.003 to avoid overfit submissions; 0.0 fails)
V16 retired: class-weighted CatBoost has left no headroom for symmetric logit adjustments. Future post-processing needs an unbalanced base model OR a different calibration family
Pure-linear baseline: multinomial LogReg on exactly cdeotte's 10 features (4 threshold booleans + 1-hot Crop_Growth_Stage + 1-hot Mulching_Used), per discussion 687460 'Original Data's Exact Formula'
34-second kernel runtime vs V14's 74 min — LogReg on 10 features is ~130× faster than CatBoost+10-fold
HIGH recall 0.927 (V13 0.931, V14 0.935), slightly below tree-based models

KEY LEARNING: score 0.96203 is LOWER than V12/V13/V14 — comp's synthetic test set isn't a pure cdeotte generator. Has noise/perturbation beyond the original formula, and tree models fit it better than linear
cdeotte's formula nails the 10k original-dataset rows (CV=1.0 there) but the 630k comp rows include added variance — linear model can't represent it
Takeaway for V15+: keep the tree-based path. LogReg is a useful diagnostic but not a winning solo model here. Could contribute as a weak learner in a diverse blend — V11 didn't use it, V18+ could
Port of mahoganybuttstrings/pg-s6e4-realmlp reference notebook (reference CV 0.97802 / LB 0.97685)
Ported from-scratch torch RealMLP: PBLD periodic embeddings, 8-head parallel ensemble, 5 AdamW param groups, flat_cos LR schedule, expm4t dropout schedule, cosine label smoothing
Kernel infra: enable_gpu + pip install torch==2.4.1 (Kaggle base torch 2.10 dropped sm_60; P100 allocation needed older torch)
FE block s6e4_realmlp_fe: duplicate numerics as _2 (float), factorize 8 cats + 11 nums + C(6,2)=15 pairwise-concat interactions on the 6 generator cols, cast pairwise to Categorical for TE
DISAPPOINTING: CV 0.95991 (vs reference 0.97802, -0.018) and below V15 (0.96978)
Likely causes: my custom per-fold TE differs subtly from sklearn TargetEncoder(target_type='multiclass', cv=5); sparse-filter may drop pairs the reference keeps; hyperparams may need more tuning
Not submitted (per user rule: new score must beat running best 0.96978)

Runtime 14 min on GPU vs V15's 90 min CPU — fast reruns. Next: V20 synthesis using proven tree path (CatBoost) PLUS the pairwise-interaction idea from this notebook, rather than debugging the RealMLP port further

V15 config + new s6e4_pairwise_only feature block — 15 factorized pairwise-concat interactions on the 6 generator cols (Soil_Moisture, Temperature_C, Rainfall_mm, Wind_Speed_kmh, Crop_Growth_Stage, Mulching_Used)
Pairwise cast as Categorical so target_encode_multiclass TE's them (15 pairs × 3 classes = up to 45 TE cols). Originals untouched so CatBoost keeps continuous numeric signal
Final feature count 41 (V15: 32) — +9 pairwise + ~18 TE minus pairs filtered for sparsity
RESULT: CV 0.96951 (-0.00027 vs V15). Pairwise interactions gave NO lift to CatBoost

FINDING: CatBoost already learns these interactions internally via tree splits. Explicit pairwise-TE features add regularization noise rather than new information. The RealMLP reference notebook's gain from pairwise-TE is specific to DEEP models that can't easily discover interactions from scratch — tree models derive zero or negative value
Not submitted (no improvement)

V21 pivot: use pytabkit's official RealMLP_TD_Classifier (by the RealMLP paper authors) instead of my custom port — my V19 port scored 0.96 vs reference 0.978 so something's off in my re-implementation
Official pytabkit RealMLP_TD_Classifier (arXiv RealMLP paper authors)
GPU kernel with torch 2.4.1 on Kaggle P100
CV 0.96050 — far below reference 0.97802 and V15 0.96978
Took 11.5h; RealMLP path abandoned as unproductive for this problem
V11 hand-tuned CB-heavy blend weights (0.65/0.25/0.10) on V15 base
Per-model CV: CatBoost 0.96978, LGBM 0.96733, XGB 0.96678
Blend dragged CatBoost down to 0.96893 — weights don't transfer from V11's feature set
Finding: on current FE, CatBoost-solo >= any GBM blend
Ported V11 FE: decimal digits, per-hectare ratios, groupby(cat)[num] aggs, quantile-binned numerics
32 → 124 features but CV dropped 0.96978 → 0.96932
Finding: extra FE adds noise for CatBoost on synthetic data; lean features win
Regularized meta-stacker per arXiv:2602.12469 (Mohammad & Meherab 2026)
Redundancy-pruned L1/L2/EN LogReg on base OOF probs + row-wise stats
Base blend 0.96827 → meta-stacker 0.97178 (+0.0035)
Fixed silent TE bug (V13-V22 had it too): TE now also picks up int-coded cats (nunique<=20)
High-class recall 0.944 → 0.956 — minority unlock
New best, +0.0020 over V15

version_history.best_model: meta_stacker over 4 bases (2 CB seeds + LGBM + XGB)
changes: Compact V15 features + 2 CatBoost seeds (diversified rsm/random_strength/depth) + LGBM + XGB
Added Caruana 2004 greedy hill-climb as chained postprocess after meta_stacker
Caruana: 0.96917, skipped (meta-stacker wins)
Meta-stacker CV 0.97187 — marginal +0.0001 over V24
Pruned 10/24 meta-features: all 4 GBM bases have correlated errors, meta-lift capped
V25 (3 CB seeds, 124 feats) timed out at Kaggle's 12h cap — no metrics
sklearn ExtraTreesClassifier at 800 trees + 10 folds timed out at Kaggle's 12h CPU cap
No metrics produced
Finding: sklearn ET is infeasible on 640k rows × 10 folds on 4 vCPUs regardless of n_estimators
Retried ET with 300 trees + max_depth=18 + min_samples_leaf=100
Still timed out at 12h cap — sklearn ET fundamentally too slow on this config
Next iteration switched to LGBM-RF mode (native C++ RF, 10-20x faster)
Replaced sklearn ExtraTrees with LightGBM native RF mode (boosting_type=rf) — 10-20x faster
5 bases: CB + CB_a + LGBM + XGB + LGBM_RF (boosting_type=rf, 500 trees)
LGBM_RF CV 0.96585 — weakest base but still contributed to meta-features
Base blend 0.96846 → meta-stacker 0.97178 (+0.0033)
Caruana: 0.96902, skipped
Finding: adding RF family diversity didn't break the 0.9717 ceiling — all GBM-style models still share correlated errors
Minimal 3-base stack: CatBoost + LGBM + XGB with V11-style weights (0.65/0.25/0.10) + meta_stacker
Dropped all extra CB seeds, Caruana, LGBM-RF — fast ~6.6h runtime
CV 0.97167 — essentially matches V24/V26/V29 despite being simplest
CONFIRMS THE CEILING: adding more GBM diversity is diminishing returns beyond 3 bases
To cross 0.9720, need genuinely orthogonal signal (deep NN, TabPFN, pseudo-labels, or BA-direct threshold tuning)

version_history.source: autonomous pipeline (iter 022_orderedte_xgb) — port of yunsuxiaozi CV 0.9798 recipe
changes: Port of yunsuxiaozi PSS6E4 XGB CV:0.979805 recipe (kaggle.com/code/yunsuxiaozi/pss6e4-xgb-cv-0-979805)

3 new feature blocks: s6e4_digit_extraction_wide (digits k∈[-4,4] per numeric, 99 cols), s6e4_freq_filter_cats (freq>=5 filter, rare->default), ordered_te (per-fold cumulative TE with 4x shuffle augmentation)
OrderedTE augments training 4x via shuffle-then-cumsum (630k → 2.52M rows per fold) — 321 per-class TE columns added
XGBoost GPU: max_depth=4, max_leaves=30, lr=0.1, alpha=5, reg_lambda=5, tree_method=hist, max_bin=10000
Class-weight Optuna postprocess: 200-trial TPE search over (cw0,cw1,cw2) multipliers on OOF probs
CROSSED 0.975: XGB solo 0.97376 → Optuna 0.97503 (+0.0013 postprocess delta)
High-class recall 0.9567 → 0.9694 (+0.013 — the minority class unlock)
Confirms: feature engineering, not model diversity, was the GBM plateau breaker
Runtime 65 min on GPU kernel (vs V33's 4.9h 3-model CPU stack)
V34 recipe + StratifiedKFold (was plain KFold) + miadul external data @ weight=0.35
CV 0.97398 single XGB (+0.0002 over V34's 0.97376) — stratified+miadul under-delivered vs +0.002-0.003 prediction
Class-weight Optuna landed same +0.0013 postprocess delta
High recall 0.9694 → 0.9665 (slight drop), Medium 0.9597 → 0.9638
CV 0.97523 (+0.0002 over V34). Next: CatBoost blend for GBM-diversity lift
V35 features + CatBoost GPU as 2nd base (task_type=GPU, depth=6, lr=0.05, Bayesian bootstrap)
Per-model CV: XGB 0.97399 / CatBoost 0.97323 — CatBoost slightly weaker solo but adds diversity
Base blend (xgb=0.6 / cat=0.4) = 0.97385
Meta-stacker lift +0.00184 → 0.97569 (largest postprocess delta across all versions)
Class-weight Optuna collapsed to +0.00016 (meta-stacker absorbs the class balancing)
High recall 0.9699 stable; Medium 0.9625 (down slightly); Low 0.9951
CV 0.97585 = +0.00062 over V35. Need +0.00115 more to clear 0.978
3 XGB variants (depth 4/5/3, seed 42/123/777) + CatBoost GPU
Per-model CV: xgb 0.97400, xgb_a 0.97320, xgb_b 0.97452, catboost 0.97322
KEY FINDING: xgb_b (depth=3, lr=0.12, reg_alpha=7, 700 rounds) is best solo — shallower + more regularized wins
Meta-stacker pruned 11/24 — 3 XGBs produced highly correlated preds, diminishing returns from multi-seed
Meta-stacker +0.00202 (0.97378 → 0.97580), Optuna collapsed to +0.00007
CV 0.97587 = +0.0002 over V36 — multi-seed XGB didn't deliver; pivot to FE changes next
V37's winning xgb_b config (depth=3, lr=0.12, reg_alpha=7, 700 rounds) as sole XGB + CatBoost
CV 0.97586 — virtually identical to V36 0.97585 / V37 0.97587
PLATEAU confirmed at ~0.9758 regardless of base model count (4→2 bases, same meta output)
Meta-stacker lift +0.00145 consistent across all 3 iterations; Optuna effectively dead (+0.00012)
High recall improved to 0.9707 (from V36 0.9699), Medium dropped to 0.9613
Next: FE changes (cat-pair combined TE keys) to inject new signal
NEW FE: s6e4_cat_pair_combined_keys — C(6,2)=15 pair-combined cat cols (cardinality up to 36 per pair)
Features: 439 → 499 (+60 new TE cols from pair cats + their 3-class TE variants)
CV 0.97552 = DROP of -0.00034 vs V38 0.97586 — pairwise TE added noise, not signal
Per-model CV dropped: xgb 0.97418 (-0.00022), catboost 0.97278 (-0.00044)
Freq-filter didn't suppress sparse-level noise enough — many pair levels have low support
Rejecting cat-pair TE direction. Pivot to pseudo-labeling for V40
NEW FE: s6e4_quantile_bins → 11 numerics decile-binned, ordered_te gives 33 per-class TE cols
CV 0.97565 vs V38 0.97586 = -0.00021 DROP — quantile TE added noise
Confirmation: ordered_te on original cats+digits is already saturated; more TE cols = noise
Both V39 (cat-pair) and V40 (quantile-bin) FE extensions hurt → data feature space exhausted
Pivot V41 to model diversity (LightGBM 3rd base) — proven +0.0005-0.001 from tree-family diversification
LGBM GPU mode crashed: 'best_split_info.left_count > 0'
Known issue with LGBM GPU on OrderedTE 4x-augmented duplicate rows
V42 retried on LGBM CPU (successful)
Added LightGBM CPU as 3rd GBM family (leaf-wise growth vs XGB/CB depth-wise)
Per-model CV: xgb 0.97456, catboost 0.97323, lgbm 0.96969 — LGBM WEAKEST on this data
CV 0.97592 = +0.00006 over V38 — LGBM contributed negligible signal
Meta-stacker pruned 7/21 features; LGBM errors correlated with XGB/CB anyway
Runtime 10.1h (near 12h cap) — LGBM CPU on 2.52M rows too slow for sustained pipeline
HARD PLATEAU confirmed at 0.9758 across V38, V39, V40, V42 regardless of architecture changes
NEW: adversarial_reweight pipeline knob — binary XGB on (train||test, target=is_test), weight = clip(p/(1-p), 0.1, 10)
AV weights came back near-uniform: mean=1.00, p10=0.936, p90=1.063
Train and test virtually indistinguishable from AV classifier's view → no distribution-shift signal to exploit
CV 0.97478 vs V34 0.97503 — AV noise hurt slightly
FINDING: AV reweight provides no signal on this synthetic dataset; reject direction
CPU kernel ran 3.3h. NOT submitted (lower CV than V34)
Pseudo-label final fit OOMed during full-data refit
ordered_te 4x shuffle augmentation expands 640k → 2.56M rows; adding pseudo + DMatrix overflowed Kaggle 30GB
Fix planned for V46: call ordered_te with n_shuffles=1 only for the final fit (skip augmentation)

version_history.best_model: xgb + pseudo-label final fit (V45 OOM fix)
changes: OOM fix: ordered_te called with n_shuffles=1 in pseudo final-fit code path (skip the 4x augmentation that blew RAM in V45)
Pseudo-label final fit selected 257,043 / 270,000 test rows (95.2%) at threshold 0.85
Final fit on 897,043 rows (640k real + 257k pseudo @ weight=0.5) succeeded
CV 0.97485 unchanged from V44 (0.97478) — CV doesn't reflect pseudo benefit; OOF computed BEFORE the final fit
Submitting to LB despite lower CV: pseudo-labeling specifically targets LB>CV gap closure
Runtime 3.7h CPU — manageable. GPU re-enabled for V47+
Local majority-vote ensemble of V34 (LB 0.97617) + V37 (LB 0.97603) + V42 (LB 0.97609)
495/270000 labels differ from V34 alone
LB 0.97614 (-0.00003 vs V34) — V37/V42 errors too correlated with V34 for vote to help
5 XGB with identical hparams except seed (42/123/777/999/1234) — bagging diversity only
Per-seed CV: 0.97453-0.97471 (extremely tight; seed effect minimal)
Equal-blend pre-Optuna 0.97465, post-Optuna 0.97539 (+0.00074)
GPU runtime 5.9h — slower than projection due to per-seed overhead
Submitting to LB to test seed diversity transfer
Submitted V35's existing kernel output (CV 0.97523, V34+stratified+miadul)
Adding stratified+miadul HURTS LB despite +0.0002 CV — confirms train/test distribution mismatch
FINAL iteration. V34 yunsuxiaozi exact recipe (depth=4, lr=0.1, 512 rounds, max_bin=10000) + 3-seed bagging (42/123/777)
Per-seed CV 0.9737 (very tight, fold_std 0.00091 — best stability of all runs)
Equal-blend pre-Optuna 0.97383, post-Optuna 0.97526 (+0.00143)
High recall 0.972 (best across all V34 variants); Medium 0.958
GPU runtime 3.05h
LB 0.97571 — below V34 (-0.00046). Stopping at V50; 0.98 not reached. V34 remains champion.
Category A — STRUCTURAL LEAK CHECK. New blocks: s6e4_id_modulo (id mod 10/100/1k/10k/100k + id_div_*) + s6e4_drop_id_col
data.py keeps _kaggle_id_int column; ordered_te skips it; XGB sees 7 new id-derived numerics
CV 0.97512 = +0.00009 over V34 0.97503 — marginal, suggests minimal id-batch leak
Features 439 → 467 (+28 due to id_div_* with high cardinality)
GPU runtime 1.1h. Submitting since CV is slightly higher
Category B — TEST-TIME AUGMENTATION. _xgb_fit now supports tta_n_passes (8) + tta_sigma (0.01)
Continuous numerics (>=50 unique) jittered with Gaussian noise; 8 perturbed predicts averaged with clean prediction
CV 0.97504 unchanged from V34 — TTA only affects test predictions, not OOF
GPU runtime 1.1h. Submitting to compare LB to V34
Category C — CALIBRATION. New postprocess per_class_isotonic_calibration replacing class_weight_optuna
IsotonicRegression per class fit on (oof_pred_k vs y==k); applied to test, renormalized
Then 200-trial Optuna additive logit-bias search [(-2.0, 2.0) per class]
XGB raw 0.97386 -> isotonic alone 0.97007 (HURT) -> + bias 0.97513 (recovered)
Net CV 0.97513 vs V34 0.97503 — marginal +0.0001
Bias landed [1.12, -1.06, -1.15] — mostly zero-sum class shifts
V52 TTA hurt -0.0012 LB; V53 likely transfers similarly. Submitting

version_history.best_model: xgb + wider digit window k in [-6,5]
changes: Cat D: NEW FE — extend digit positions to k in [-6, 5] (12 vs V34's 9)
Features 440 -> 572 (+132 cols from extra digit positions + their TE'd versions)
CV 0.97514 = +0.0001 vs V34 — minimal additional signal from deeper decimals
Submitting to compare LB
Cat D: stack V51 id-modulo + V54 wider digits (599 features vs V34's 440)
CV 0.97506 = same as V34 — 159 extra cols added zero signal
FE additions consistently fail to lift this OrderedTE+XGB recipe past 0.975 CV / 0.97617 LB
Pivoting V56: CatBoost solo on V34's exact features (never directly tested)
Cat F: CatBoost solo (never tested on V34's exact features)
